tool_input.summary:
Cassini observations indicate tail reconnection at Saturn is most often seen in the *nightside plasma sheet/current sheet*, preferentially *near midnight to post‑midnight (dawnward) local times*, at *mid‑tail distances of roughly ~20–40 RS* (Saturn radii) downtail (i.e., negative X in KSM/KSO-type coordinates). Note that the apparent “most frequent” region is influenced by where Cassini spent the most time sampling the tail current sheet.

If you want to reproduce/quantify the occurrence distribution (e.g., via reconnection signatures such as plasmoids, dipolarizations, Hall fields, high-speed flows, or electron/ion jets), the PDS4 collections below are the most directly useful (calibrated MAG + calibrated CAPS).

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary calibrated magnetic-field time series in a Saturn-tail-relevant coordinate system (KSM). This is typically the backbone dataset for identifying reconnection signatures (plasmoids, dipolarizations, Bz reversals, etc.) and mapping where they occur in the tail.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Highest time-resolution calibrated MAG data at Saturn (KRTP). Useful for resolving short-duration reconnection structures (flux ropes/plasmoids, current sheet crossings) once candidate intervals are found.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Electron Spectrometer Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated electron distributions to accompany MAG when diagnosing reconnection (electron heating/acceleration, boundary identification, plasma sheet vs lobe, etc.).

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Ion Beam Spectrometer Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated ion measurements for reconnection-associated flows/jets and plasma sheet characterization, complementary to MAG.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI LEMMS Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle enhancements/injections can provide supporting context for reconnection-driven events and substorm-like activity in the tail.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
This node has NO mission layer. ~767 datasets sit directly under data/.
Do NOT call pds_list_missions — it will return an empty list.
Instead, go directly to pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') using mission or instrument keywords to narrow the listing.
Then probe the most relevant directories with pds_probe_datasets.


Common mission/instrument keywords:
  Cassini → cassini (instruments: CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, UVIS, INMS, CDA, RADAR)
  Galileo → galileo (instruments: EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR)
  Juno → juno (instruments: Waves, JADE, JEDI, MAG, MWR, UVS)
  Voyager 1/2 → voyager1, voyager2 (instruments: CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS)
  New Horizons → new-horizons (instruments: PEPSSI, SWAP, SDC)
  Pioneer 10/11 → pioneer10, pioneer11 (instruments: CPI, GTT, MAG, PA)
  MESSENGER → messenger (instruments: EPPS, MAG, NS)
  Ulysses → ulysses (instruments: SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, SWICS, COSPIN)
  MAVEN → maven (instruments: MAG, NGIMS, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, LPW)
  THEMIS → themis (instruments: ESA, SST, FGM, SCM, EFI)

Dataset naming: directories often follow the pattern '<mission>-<target>-<instrument>-<description>' (e.g. 'cassini-caps-calibrated').
Use the filter parameter on list_dataset_dirs to search by mission or instrument name.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') to find datasets.
  Step 2: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths.
  Step 3: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections.
  Step 4: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
Where in Saturn's magnetotail is reconnection observed most frequently?

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J. H.; Wilson, R. J.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle consists of all of the calibrated data acquired by            
                Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) on-board the Cassini spacecraft      
                during the Cassini mission.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer raw magnetic field data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                magnetic-field data from the Fluxgate Magnetometer and the Vector Helium Magnetomer in Vector mode.
                Data are received in science and housekeeping rates. Additional collections, such as spacecraft attitude, analog, 
                command validation, configuration image, error counter, user-defined engineering data, browse plots, and calibration,
                are also included.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

parts.args: {"summary":"Cassini observations indicate tail reconnection at Saturn is most often seen in the *nightside plasma sheet/current sheet*, preferentially *near midnight to post‑midnight (dawnward) local times*, at *mid‑tail distances of roughly ~20–40 RS* (Saturn radii) downtail (i.e., negative X in KSM/KSO-type coordinates). Note that the apparent “most frequent” region is influenced by where Cassini spent the most time sampling the tail current sheet.\n\nIf you want to reproduce/quantify the occurrence distribution (e.g., via reconnection signatures such as plasmoids, dipolarizations, Hall fields, high-speed flows, or electron/ion jets), the PDS4 collections below are the most directly useful (calibrated MAG + calibrated CAPS).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-1sec-ksm","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection","mission":"Cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary calibrated magnetic-field time series in a Saturn-tail-relevant coordinate system (KSM). This is typically the backbone dataset for identifying reconnection signatures (plasmoids, dipolarizations, Bz reversals, etc.) and mapping where they occur in the tail."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-full-krtp","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection","mission":"Cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Highest time-resolution calibrated MAG data at Saturn (KRTP). Useful for resolving short-duration reconnection structures (flux ropes/plasmoids, current sheet crossings) once candidate intervals are found."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-calibrated:data-els","path":"data/cassini-caps-calibrated/","title":"Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Electron Spectrometer Data Collection","mission":"Cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Calibrated electron distributions to accompany MAG when diagnosing reconnection (electron heating/acceleration, boundary identification, plasma sheet vs lobe, etc.)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-calibrated:data-ibs","path":"data/cassini-caps-calibrated/","title":"Cassini-Huygens Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Calibrated Ion Beam Spectrometer Data Collection","mission":"Cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Calibrated ion measurements for reconnection-associated flows/jets and plasma sheet characterization, complementary to MAG."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mimi-lemms-cal","path":"data/cassini-mimi-lemms-cal/","title":"Cassini MIMI LEMMS Calibrated Bundle","mission":"Cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Energetic particle enhancements/injections can provide supporting context for reconnection-driven events and substorm-like activity in the tail."}]}
